  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",

    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",

    "RX.3",

    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To “make this stronger,” which scope should I prioritize: (A) micro-level writing polish only; (B) macro-level reframing of the contribution and novelty positioning (without changing reported results); or (C) both (B) plus tight evidence–claim alignment that may adjust claim strength to avoid overstatement?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening likely hinges on reframing the thesis and contribution so the parameter-sharing innovation and practical impact are foregrounded.",
    "R1.2": "A stronger writeup requires a coherent premise-to-conclusion chain (e.g., shared-parameter regularization benefiting sparse edge types).",
    "R1.3": "Claims about accuracy and parameter reductions must align precisely with the reported numbers to be compelling without overreach.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level clarity, emphasis, and concision are central to making the prose read as more confident and impactful.",
    "RX.1": "The brief request is ambiguous, so explicitly acknowledging scope uncertainty is necessary.",
    "RX.2": "Because the reported accuracy deltas are small, avoiding exaggerated claims is critical.",
    "RX.3": "Scope adherence ensures this remains a responsibility projection rather than editing or altering the artifact.",
    "RX.4": "Guarding against inflated scientific claims helps prevent downstream misunderstanding or misuse.",
    "RX.5": "Maintaining clear linkage from strengthened claims to the cited sections and reported metrics preserves traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To “make this stronger,” which scope should I prioritize: (A) micro-level writing polish only; (B) macro-level reframing of the contribution and novelty positioning (without changing reported results); or (C) both (B) plus tight evidence–claim alignment that may adjust claim strength to avoid overstatement?

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening likely hinges on reframing the thesis and contribution so the parameter-sharing innovation and practical impact are foregrounded.
rationale.R1.2: A stronger writeup requires a coherent premise-to-conclusion chain (e.g., shared-parameter regularization benefiting sparse edge types).
rationale.R1.3: Claims about accuracy and parameter reductions must align precisely with the reported numbers to be compelling without overreach.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity, emphasis, and concision are central to making the prose read as more confident and impactful.
rationale.RX.1: The brief request is ambiguous, so explicitly acknowledging scope uncertainty is necessary.
rationale.RX.2: Because the reported accuracy deltas are small, avoiding exaggerated claims is critical.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence ensures this remains a responsibility projection rather than editing or altering the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Guarding against inflated scientific claims helps prevent downstream misunderstanding or misuse.
rationale.RX.5: Maintaining clear linkage from strengthened claims to the cited sections and reported metrics preserves traceability.
